templates.title: New Site Onboarding SOP
templates.description: Standard operating procedure for onboarding a new site into the json-directory starter using the checked-in site contract.

items.title: Lock the site identity
items.description: Confirm the stable domain, repo, and internal site id before creating any files.

subItems.title: Use the domain as the site id when possible, for example `browserextensions.io`.
Confirm the publish repo owner/name and branch.
Confirm the final public URL, not a placeholder.

items.title: Lock the public naming and route vocabulary
items.description: Decide what the site calls its main collection and which public route bases it will keep stable.

contents.value:
**Decide before launch:**

- `copy.listingName.singular` and `copy.listingName.plural`
- `routes.listingBasePath`
- `routes.docsBasePath`
- `routes.networkBasePath`
- whether docs, posts, and network are public at launch

items.title: Create the checked-in site folder
items.description: Add the new site directory under `sites/<site-id>/` and keep the contract sparse.

subItems.title: Create `sites/<site-id>/site-content.ts` if the site has owned resources or network links
Create `sites/<site-id>/README.md` explaining the source of truth and local commands
Add any checked-in assets under `sites/<site-id>/assets/` if the site needs local branding files

items.title: Register the site in the runtime
items.description: Wire the new site into the repo so validation, dev, and builds can resolve it.

contents.value:
**Before promotion into the active registry:**

- set `build.appPackageName` to `<site-id>` in `sites/<site-id>/site-config.ts`
- set `build.appOutDir` to `apps/<site-id>/out` in `sites/<site-id>/site-config.ts`
- update `packages/site-contract/src/index.ts`
- update `packages/site-contract/src/active-site-ids.ts`
- update any site-specific docs or examples if the contract changed
- update issue/project tracking if this onboarding is tied to a tracked rollout

items.title: Choose the source-of-truth format
items.description: Decide whether the site edits normalized listing JSON directly or uses the adapter-driven products source.

subItems.title: Use `listing-json` when maintainers should edit normalized listing records directly.
Use the adapter-driven `products.json` path when the site edits `sites/<site-id>/products.json` and the current source adapter regenerates `data/listings.json`.

items.title: Shape the listing data to the active contract
items.description: Use the lean listing contract the page actually renders.

contents.value:
**Current lean contract:**

- `product.title`
- `product.slug`
- `product.tagline`
- `product.productPage`
- optional `product.categories` (first item becomes the canonical route category)
- optional `media.logo`, `media.images`, `media.video`
- optional `content.body`, `content.faq`
- optional `relatedLinks[]`

Keep implementation-only fields out of the source contract.

items.title: Generate the runtime listing dataset
items.description: Run the site-prep flow so the app reads the latest checked-in data.

items.title: Run the site validator
items.description: Fail early on bad route inputs, unknown category slugs, and invalid listing data.

items.title: Regenerate the search index after data changes
items.description: Refresh the checked-in search artifact whenever listing data changes.

items.title: Start the site in local dev mode
items.description: Use the site-aware dev command so the shell resolves the intended site config and listing data.

items.title: Smoke-test the core public pages
items.description: Verify the user-facing routes and navigation, not just tests.

subItems.title: Homepage renders the correct site name, tagline, and listing terminology
At least one listing detail page renders the hero, bottom links, and category links correctly
At least one category page loads at `/categories/<slug>` and only shows populated categories
Search returns expected listings and category filters
Legal/footer links point to the intended pages and copy

items.title: Check optional feature surfaces
items.description: Verify that docs, posts, newsletter, and network are either intentionally enabled or absent.

contents.value: Confirm the site matches its feature flags. If a surface is off by default, make sure it does not leak into navigation, sitemap, or empty pages.

items.title: Build the final static artifact
items.description: Generate the deployable output for the new site and inspect the final route shape in `dist/`.

items.title: Inspect the final artifact, not just the raw Next route log
items.description: The final public route contract is the post-processed site artifact in `dist/sites/<site-id>`.

subItems.title: Confirm the listing route base under `dist/sites/<site-id>/<listing-base>/`
Confirm `search/search-index.json` exists and matches the site data
Confirm `sitemap-index.xml` and split sitemap files exist as expected

items.title: Update issue, project board, and notes
items.description: Close the loop with a recorded outcome and any follow-up gaps.

contents.value:
**Before calling onboarding done:**

- update the linked GitHub issue with what changed
- move the project card to the correct status
- document any newly discovered contract gaps in `docs/knowledge/**` or split follow-up issues
